copy.title: Tigers walk off Rays in ten. Brewers obliterate Giants. Knicks-Spurs tips tomorrow night.
copy.keyTakeaway: Baseball had some tight finishes yesterday—Tigers edged the Rays, Rockies walked off the Angels—while the NBA Finals tip off tomorrow with Knicks-Spurs. Markets mixed: tech took hits (Microsoft -4.2%, Dell -6.6%), but Apple and Tesla bounced back.

todaysHits.sports: Tigers walk it off. Rockies too. Knicks-Spurs tomorrow.
todaysHits.markets: Tech stumbles. SPY flat. Bitcoin down 5.7%.
todaysHits.golf: Brian Campbell leads Memorial Tournament early.
todaysHits.worldcup: World Cup opens June 11. 0 matches played.
todaysHits.culture: Taylor Swift writes Toy Story 5 song. Dua Lipa married. Streets of Rage movie incoming.

f1.headline: Monaco's tight streets punish mistakes. Max knows this.
f1.whyCare1: Monaco separates the genuinely skilled drivers from the rest—there's nowhere to hide on these barriers.
f1.whyCare2: Championship positioning matters, but Monaco is pure driver skill, no setup wizardry or tire strategy saves you here.
f1.watchFor: First-lap incidents in Turn 1. The field gets compressed early and desperation shows.
f1.whatToSay: Monaco doesn't care how fast your car is. One lock-up and you're done.

culture.topic: Taylor Swift writes Toy Story 5 song
culture.whatHappened: Swift created an original track for the upcoming Toy Story 5 film.
culture.whyItMatters: She's basically the default move for any studio that needs a song to matter culturally — Toy Story just got the Swift treatment, which means this movie has mainstream oxygen before it drops.
culture.whatToSay: Even Pixar knows you can't compete without her.

culture.topic: Browns trading Myles Garrett to Rams
culture.whatHappened: Cleveland is dealing Garrett to Los Angeles in exchange for Jared Verse and additional assets.
culture.whyItMatters: Garrett's a generational pass rusher and the Browns just decided to blow it up — this signals a full rebuild, not a playoff retool.
culture.whatToSay: The Rams got a window to compete, but the Browns basically admitted they're done trying.

culture.whatHappened: Eddie Redmayne as a freelance assassin — methodical, charming, completely untraceable — until one investigator gets close.
